Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires

[ probabilistic logic programming ]

pedro.zuidbergdosmartires@cs.kuleuven.be

about

I am currently a PhD student under the supervion of Luc De Raedt in the DTAI Lab at the KU Leuven.

In my research I focus on probabilistic programming in general, and more specifically on probabilistic logic programming. Probabilistic programing allows one to coherently incorporate uncertainty and noise in data science and machine learning models. However, general inference in probabilistic programs is a computationally hard task. I try to figure out smart ways of combining recent advances from different fields, such as knowledge compilation, symbolic inference and deep Bayesian modeling to alleviate this hardness.

Within the ReGROUND project, I also investigate the application of probabilistic logic programming to the field of robotics. As the sensor data through which a robot perceives the world is inherently noisy, robotics is predestined for probabilistic programming. Adding a logic component to it, allows a robot to reason about the world on a symbolic level while using raw sensor data.

Here is also a short non-academic CV of mine.

publications

  • Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires , Samuel Kolb. Monte Carlo Anti-Differentiation for Approximate Weighted Model Integration. In 9th workshop on Statistical Relational Artificial Intelligence StarAI (at AAAI 2020). [pdf]
  • Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires , Vincent Derkinderen, Robin Manhaeve, Wannes Meert, Angelika Kimmig, Luc De Raedt. Transforming Probabilistic Programs into Algebraic Circuits for Inference and Learning. Program Transformations for ML Workshop @ NeurIPS (2019). [pdf]
  • Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires. Differentiation and Weighted Model Integration. DeCoDeML Workshop @ ECML/PKDD (2019). [pdf, poster, slides]
  • Samuel Kolb, Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Luc De Raedt. How to Exploit Structure while Solving Weighted Model Integration Problems. In Proceedings of the Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI) (2019). [pdf, poster]
  • Samuel Kolb, Paolo Morettin, Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Francesco Sommavilla, Andrea Passerini, Roberto Sebastiani, Luc De Raedt. The pywmi Framework and Toolbox for Probabilistic Inference using Weighted Model Integration. In Proceedings of the Twenty-Eighth International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence Demos (IJCAI) (2019). [pdf]
  • Ozan Arkan Can, Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Andreas Persson, Julian Gaal, Amy Loutfi, Luc De Raedt, Deniz Yuret, Alessandro Saffiotti. Learning from Implicit Information in Natural Language Instructions for Robotic Manipulations. In Proceedings of the Combined Workshop on Spatial Language Understanding (SpLU) and Grounded Communication for Robotics (RoboNLP) (2019). [pdf]
  • Andreas Persson, Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Luc De Raedt, Amy Loutfi. Semantic Relational Object Tracking. In IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems (2019). [pdf]
  • Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Anton Dries, Luc De Raedt. Exact and Approximate Weighted Model Integration with Probability Density Functions Using Knowledge Compilation. In Proceedings of the 30th Conference on Artificial Intelligence (2019). [pdf, poster, slides]
  • Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Sebastijan Dumancic. Reactive Probabilistic Programming. In 1st International Conference on Probabilistic Programming (2018). [pdf, poster]
  • Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Anton Dries, Luc De Raedt. Knowledge Compilation with Continuous Random Variables and its Application in Hybrid Probabilistic Logic Programming. In 8th workshop on Statistical Relational Artificial Intelligence StarAI (at IJCAI/ICML 2018). [pdf, poster]
  • Laura Antanas, Ozan Arkan Can, Jesse Davis, Luc De Raedt, Amy Loutfi, Andreas Persson, Alessandro Saffiotti, Emre Unal, Deniz Yuret, Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires. Relational Symbol Grounding through Affordance Learning: An Overview of the ReGround Project. In GLU 2017 International Workshop on Grounding Language Understanding. [pdf]

software

Here is some open source software I have been involved with:

  • as developer:
    • SDD.jl: A Julia wrapper for the SDD package.
    • volestipy: A python wrapper for the VolEsti C++ library to compute volumes of convex bodies and draw samples from them.
    • HAL-ProbLog: A probabilistic programming language that extends ProbLog with continuous random variables that I am currently developing. The language is currently in alpha.
    • pywmi: I have also contributed to the pywmi toolbox, which allows you to solve weighted model integration problems using different solvers.
    • psipy: A python wrapper for the probabilistic symbolic inference engine used in the PSI-Solver.
    • PyDC: A python wrapper for the probabilistic programming language Distributional Clauses.
    • MathGenGraph: A small tool that lets you find out about your academic genealogy by scraping the Mathematics Genealogy Project.
  • as maintainer:
    • Distributional Clauses: I am currently the maintainer of this probabilistic programming language developed by Davide Nitti.

didactic tasks

  • course taught as teaching assistant:
    • Uncertainty in Artificila Intelligence (2016-2017, 2017-2018, 2018-2019)
    • Information Structures and Implications (2016-2017, 2017-2018)
  • master students supervised:
    • Michiel Baptist (2018-2019): A Genetic Algorithm to Learn the Structure of Efficient Probabilistic Graphical Models.
    • Pieter-Jan Coenen (2018-2019): Relational Language and Robot Control.
    • Evert Heylen (2018-2019): Speed-up learning for optimalisation of SDD compilation.
    • Rei Bardhi (2018-2019): Learning Transition Rules for a Relational World.
    • Olivier Kamers (2017-2018): Reward shaping in HYPE: case study op Torens van Hanoi (including video)
    • Niels Wéry (2016-2017): Anomalie detectie voor de Large Hadron Collider
  • interns supervised:
    • Shani Vanlerberghe (Summer 2019): Developed a robot platform that uses vision and language as input to reason about the world. [link]
    • Ivan Miošić (Summer 2019): Worked on weighted model integration and optimization.

volunteering

I have been a project leader in the Summer School of Science in Croatia. The summer school is a ten day event during which a small group of high school students works out their own research project and present their research to peers. I have lead two such projects:

  • 2018: Prediction of Future Behaviour of Cryptocurrency-Prices Using Machine Learning.
  • 2017: Discovering the Laws of Physics with Artificial Intelligence.

If you know, or have, a high-schooler interested in science, let them know about this great opportunity!
